title: Results Display — Dynamic Columns with Metrics
goal: Implement the side-by-side results display showing each deployment's output, token usage, estimated cost, latency, and error state in dynamically rendered Streamlit columns. Wire the Submit button to trigger batch inference and cost calculation.

in_scope: On submit, call run_batch_inference() with selected deployments and prompt
Calculate estimated cost for each result using calculate_cost()
Create one Streamlit column per selected deployment
Display in each column: deployment name (header), raw text output, input tokens, output tokens, total tokens, estimated cost, latency in ms
Display error message in error-state columns instead of output/metrics
Handle missing token data (display 'N/A' when tokens are None)
Handle missing cost data (display 'N/A' when pricing unavailable)
Show a spinner or progress indicator while inference is running
out_of_scope: Markdown rendering of output text (raw text only per PRD)

inputs: Session state: selected deployments, prompt text (from Part 06)
PRD user stories GH-005, GH-006, GH-007, GH-008
outputs: src/app.py — updated with results display section and inference trigger
dependencies: Part 06 — UI layout with session state for selections and prompt
checklist: Import run_batch_inference from src/inference.py in app.py
Import calculate_cost from src/pricing.py in app.py
On Submit button click, display st.spinner('Running inference...') while processing
Call run_batch_inference(client, selected_deployments, prompt) and store results in session_state
After inference completes, create N columns using st.columns(len(results))
For each result/column pair, render the deployment_name as a subheader (st.subheader)
If result has an error (error is not None): display st.error(result['error']) and skip metrics
If result is successful: display output_text using st.text() or st.code() to prevent Markdown rendering
Display input_tokens, output_tokens, total_tokens as formatted metrics (use st.metric or formatted text)
Call calculate_cost(result['model_name'], result['input_tokens'], result['output_tokens']) for each result
Display estimated cost formatted as USD (e.g., '$0.0042') or 'N/A' if None
Display latency_ms formatted with units (e.g., '342 ms')
If token counts are None, display 'N/A' for token fields and cost
acceptance_criteria: Submitting a prompt triggers inference and displays results in side-by-side columns
Each column shows: deployment name, output text, input/output/total tokens, cost, latency
Output text is displayed as raw text without Markdown rendering
Failed deployments show their error message in the column without affecting other columns
Missing token data displays 'N/A' instead of crashing
Missing pricing data displays 'N/A' for cost
A spinner is visible during inference execution
Columns are visually aligned and readable for 1–5 deployments
review_focus: Raw text output is not passed through st.markdown() — use st.text() or st.code() to prevent rendering
Cost formatting follows a consistent pattern (e.g., 4 decimal places for small amounts)
Column widths are equal and responsive across 1–5 columns
Error state columns are visually distinct from success columns
Session state is used to persist results so they survive Streamlit reruns
risks_or_notes: Streamlit st.columns() with 5 columns on narrow screens may be cramped — consider minimum widths or scrollable containers
Long model outputs may cause columns to expand unevenly — consider using st.expander() or max-height containers for output text
Latency measurement may show high values for sequential execution — this is expected and documented
st.text() preserves whitespace but doesn't word-wrap — st.code() may be better for long outputs
definition_of_done: Results display section is fully implemented in src/app.py
All metrics (tokens, cost, latency) render correctly for success cases
Error cases display per-column error messages
N/A handling works for missing tokens and missing pricing
The end-to-end workflow (select → prompt → submit → view results) works in the browser
No Markdown rendering of model output text occurs
